home.heroTitle: Mineral Exploration in the Heart of Bolivia
home.heroSubtitle: A TSX-listed gold and silver exploration company building a 25,950-hectare portfolio of concessions in Bolivia's Ñuflo de Chávez Province, Santa Cruz Department.
home.problemStatLabel: of junior exploration companies never complete a drill program
home.problemBody: Most exploration capital is consumed by overhead and management costs — never reaching the drill rig. Capital evaporates. Shareholders lose patience. Properties are abandoned.
home.solutionBody: Active programs at all three properties. Los Clavillos has returned peak gold grades of 12.95 g/t Au across 3,129m of historical drilling, with 2025 drilling targeting open-pit reserve definition. Cerrito XXI and XXII have approved 5-year work plans totaling ~$738K in committed budgets across 3,500m of planned diamond drilling.
home.projectsSectionSubtitle: Three gold and silver exploration concessions in Santa Cruz, Bolivia.
home.esgSectionSubtitle: Maintaining full environmental compliance and zero-harm standards across all properties in Bolivia's Ñuflo de Chávez Province.
home.contactBandSubtitle: For shareholder inquiries, analyst coverage requests, or investor presentations.
home.gallerySubtitle: On the ground in Bolivia — drilling, sampling, and field work.

investorRelations.marketDataSubtitle: Gold, silver, and copper spot prices — updated in real time.
investorRelations.whyBoliviaSubtitle: Bolivia's eastern lowlands host a prolific extension of the Brazilian Precambrian Shield — the same geological terrane responsible for multi-million-ounce gold camps across the continent.
investorRelations.whyBolivia1Desc: The Brazilian Precambrian Shield in Santa Cruz hosts orogenic gold systems analogous to producing mines across Brazil, Guyana, and Suriname. Mycelium's 25,950-ha portfolio sits directly on this terrane.
investorRelations.whyBolivia2Desc: Bolivia's AJAM-administered mineral concession system grants 30-year contracts with rights to explore, develop, and produce. Los Clavillos holds contract No. AJAM/DDSC/CAM/0010/2019 — fully executed and in good standing.
investorRelations.whyBolivia3Desc: Mycelium's team has operated continuously in Bolivia since 2011 — building relationships with regulators, communities, and service providers that take years to develop. Our local presence is a durable competitive advantage.
investorRelations.whyBolivia4Desc: All three project sites are accessible by paved highway from Santa Cruz city. Los Clavillos: 180 km paved + 5.5 km gravel. Cerrito XXI & XXII: 282 km fully paved, 5 hours drive.
investorRelations.whyBolivia5Desc: Exploration costs in eastern Bolivia are substantially lower than comparable programs in Canada or Australia — allowing Mycelium to drill more metres per dollar and accelerate the path to a resource estimate.
investorRelations.whyBolivia6Desc: 10 consecutive Annual Environmental Monitoring Reports (IMA) filed without interruption at Los Clavillos — demonstrating the regulatory track record that de-risks permitting for Cerrito XXI and XXII.

about.title: About Mycelium Minerals
about.subtitle: Leadership, values, and our commitment to responsible mining.
about.ceoQuote: We built Mycelium on a foundation of direct execution — our team drills, our equipment moves ground, and our capital goes to the drill hole, not overhead.
about.executiveTeamSubtitle: Experienced operators with deep expertise in Latin American mining.
about.boardSubtitle: Independent oversight from leaders across finance, mining, and sustainability.
about.value1Desc: Delivering systematic, methodical exploration programs across all three active concessions in Santa Cruz, Bolivia.
about.value2Desc: Partnering with Bolivian communities to create lasting social and economic value.
about.value3Desc: Maintaining the highest standards of reporting and accountability as a TSX issuer.

sustainability.title: Sustainability & ESG
sustainability.subtitle: Our commitment to environmental compliance, worker safety, and responsible exploration in Bolivia's Ñuflo de Chávez Province.
sustainability.environmentalDesc: Los Clavillos operates under Declaratoria de Impacto Ambiental No. 071103/02/DIA/5936/15, granted February 6, 2015. Annual environmental monitoring is conducted by PetroLab, covering water quality and air quality across the concession area. The 10th Annual Environmental Monitoring Report (IMA 2024–2025) has been submitted to Bolivia's Ministry of Mining, Ministry of Environment, and the Santa Cruz Gobernación — demonstrating over a decade of continuous compliance.
sustainability.socialDesc: Safety is the first priority at all our exploration sites. The Los Clavillos 2025 program — which included diamond drilling, CAT 320D2L excavator operations, and trenching campaigns — recorded zero accidents across the full year. Our field team, comprising a resident geologist, specialized drill crew, three expert consultant groups, and ten field laborers, operates under formal safety protocols on every shift.
sustainability.governanceDesc: Los Clavillos is operated under a 30-year concession contract approved by AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera), No. AJAM/DDSC/CAM/0010/2019, granted February 18, 2019. Continuous patent payments have been maintained without interruption since 2013. Mycelium Minerals Bolivia MMB S.R.L. is registered with SEPREC (No. 188932027) and holds NIT No. 188932027, maintaining full regulatory compliance with Bolivian mining and environmental authorities.
sustainability.reportDesc: Our annual ESG report details environmental monitoring results, safety performance, and regulatory compliance across all properties.
